rationale.R1.1: A review of this paper section centrally includes checking whether the thesis, problem framing, and contribution claim are coherently reconstructed at the section level.
rationale.R1.2: Logical consistency is load-bearing because the section makes several technical claims whose premises, conclusions, and transitions must cohere.
rationale.R1.3: Evidence-claim alignment is central because the section invokes empirical verification and optimization guarantees that should be supported by reported results elsewhere in the paper.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty assessment is central because the section explicitly claims a first convergence rate and positions the work against prior aggregation and online learning results.
rationale.R1.5: Structural reorganization is central because reviewing a section includes assessing whether the order of claims, prior work, method, theory, and implications forms a clear narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is central because a general section review normally covers sentence-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.R1.7: Citation and scholarship is central because the section cites named prior work and makes claims about how prior aggregation methods are framed.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is active because the reviewer must mark limits caused by seeing only this section rather than the full paper or cited works.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is active because the section contains broad claims about first results, non-convex penalties, and guarantees equivalent to synthesis.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is active because the task is only to review the section, not to rewrite or execute the research agenda.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is active because inaccurate validation of technical or novelty claims could mislead later paper revision or submission decisions.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability is active because any review judgments should be traceable to statements in the provided section and distinguish them from external knowledge.
